evals.prompt: Set up a ralph loop for epic checkout-foundation (CHK01), don't start it yet.
evals.expected_output: Resolved the epic, derived a dependency-safe task sequence, resolved the environment, and called scripts/seed-ralph-loop.sh with --preset engineering-delivery and every value passed via --set. Reported files written, configuration, expected branch, and how to start.
assertions: Calls scripts/seed-ralph-loop.sh rather than hand-writing active.md
Passes --agent matching the host (claude or cursor)
Task sequence respects dependencies declared in tasks.md
Proposes max_iterations sized to the task count, not the bare default of 50
Writes into .claude/loop/ or .cursor/loop/ with no root-level pointer file
Does not start the loop, execute steps, or launch sub-agents
Does not create or switch git branches
Reports how to start the loop (/ralph-loop start)

evals.prompt: Set up a ralph loop for epic checkout-foundation.
evals.expected_output: Fails loudly, naming the missing file, and stops. Does not invent a task sequence or seed a partial loop.
assertions: Names the missing file explicitly
Does not seed any loop file
Does not fabricate tasks or acceptance criteria

evals.prompt: I want a ralph loop but not for code — I want it to research a topic and produce a briefing.
evals.expected_output: Recommends the custom preset, interviews for the step list and completion criteria, writes the steps to a file, and seeds with --preset custom --steps-file.
assertions: Does not force the engineering-delivery preset onto a non-engineering job
Asks for the steps and how each one advances
Points at references/preset-authoring.md for the step contract
Ensures every step writes durable state so the stall guard works

evals.prompt: Set up a loop with no iteration limit that runs until I say stop.
evals.expected_output: Explains that a loop with neither a completion promise nor an iteration limit is refused by the seed script, and proposes either a promise or a limit before seeding.
assertions: Does not seed an unbounded loop
Explains the hard ceiling of 200 iterations
Proposes a concrete completion promise or iteration limit

evals.prompt: Re-seed the loop for this epic.
evals.context: A loop is already active at iteration 12.
evals.expected_output: Reports that a loop is in progress at iteration 12, and asks whether to cancel it or overwrite with --force. Does not silently clobber it.
assertions: Detects the in-progress loop before seeding
Reports the current iteration
Requires explicit confirmation before overwriting
